title: Optional calibration/predictions for duty-relevant forecasts
context: Ofelia includes calibrated predictions as a personal-advantage feature. Public-Official-Guide could offer a reframed version: calibration of the official's own predictions about duty-relevant matters (e.g., how often does this type of variance get appealed, does this budget estimate typically hold) -- as honest self-assessment and service follow-through, not political-outcome optimization. This is an open question in the project plan; it may not be core to fiduciary duty. This task is optional and lower priority than the core faculties.
objective: If the project governance decides calibration is in scope, implement optional duty-relevant prediction calibration (honest self-assessment for the official, not political-outcome optimization) as a low-priority add-on behind the guardrail layer.
acceptanceCriteria: Calibration tracks only duty-relevant predictions (civic outcomes, service estimates) -- not political or electoral outcomes
Framed as honest self-assessment and service follow-through; not advantage-optimization
Mounted behind the guardrail layer; political outcome prediction is refused
Clearly optional and disableable; does not gate any core faculty
output: A merged PR adding optional duty-relevant prediction calibration behind the guardrail layer, framed as honest self-assessment, disableable, and not blocking any core faculty.
